title: notifications-sms — HTTP middleware
description: Middleware for the SMS-related HTTP surface (opt-out webhooks, opt-in confirmation endpoint, phone-number verification). The transport itself (SMS delivery to Twilio / MessageBird / etc) is server-to-provider outbound and does not need middleware; the callback endpoints do.

middleware.description: Applied to POST /api/v1/notifications/sms/callbacks/{provider}. Verifies the provider-specific HMAC / X-Signature header (Twilio: X-Twilio-Signature; MessageBird: MessageBird-Signature). Rejects with 401 on signature mismatch — prevents forged status callbacks.
Per-phone-number rate limit on opt-in code verification (5/hour, 20/day). Prevents brute-forcing the 6-digit verification code. Uses Laravel's RateLimiter facade keyed by hashed phone number.

TCPA / GDPR compliance middleware. Applied at the ChannelResolver level (not HTTP). Checks the incoming SMS body for STOP / UNSTOP / HELP keywords BEFORE delivery + short-circuits with the required auto-reply. Required by US TCPA + EU e-Privacy directive.

notes: The inbound-SMS surface (STOP handling) does NOT sit on HTTP middleware — it hooks into the ChannelResolver's dispatch pipeline. Listed here for discoverability so ops can find every SMS-related gate in one place.
